observedSectionWindows.note: Fourth Amendment title, footnote revision, and section updates.

boundaries: Keep the Fourth Amendment separate from the core Regulation 213 wave.

Keep the FAQ addendum, the Fifth Amendment addendum, the Sixth Amendment addendum, and the other amendment-history PDFs in the same folder out of this wave unless a later amendment-history plan explicitly opens them.
Preserve page locators and line references where available, but expect the text layer to remain page-image-backed for exact wording.
Keep the amendment text and certification page separate so the boundary logic stays easy to review.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No Regulation 213 core-wave extraction in this batch.
No FAQ addendum pages in this wave.
No Fifth Amendment pages in this wave.
No Sixth Amendment pages in this wave.
No other amendment-history PDFs in the same wave.

proposedBatches.title: Regulation 213 Fourth Amendment amendment text and section updates

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the certification language as one review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the certification language as one review-only slice.

reviewStandards.certifiedAmendmentText: Treat the source as certified regulatory text and not as a learner-facing explanation or a promoted artifact.
reviewStandards.stateRegulation: Treat the source as New York regulatory material tied to Regulation 213.
reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat direct references to regulatory duties as mapped regulatory context, but do not promote the amendment itself into binding text.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat incorporated-manual and certification language as documentation context if it appears.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to the Valuation Manual and the amended footnote as mapped context unless the text operationalizes them.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title and introductory text as background unless the text imposes a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat dense or ambiguous amendment wording as requiring human review unless the page image confirms the wording.

validationRequirements: The plan JSON must exist and parse cleanly.
The plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
The dedicated batch-definition file must exist and stay synchronized with the plan.
The planned batches must stay review-only by default.
The source must remain certified and not promoted.
The source must stay separate from the core Regulation 213 wave and the amendment addenda.
The source should remain page-image-backed for exact wording where needed.
